title: Check Overall System RMS For System Faults And Active AGV Faults
summary: Use the Overall System RMS page to determine whether system faults or active AGV faults are present during support troubleshooting. The source states this should be an initial support question and identifies where each fault type appears on the page.
when_to_use: Use during support troubleshooting when determining whether faults are currently visible in the Overall System RMS view, especially as an initial question on a support call.
not_for: Interpreting the meaning of specific faults beyond confirming whether they are shown
Using as a substitute for direct screen access when no one on the call can view or share the page

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Ask whether system faults are present and guide the review of the Overall System RMS page.
If they have access, view or share the Overall System RMS page and report what is visible.

safety_notes: This source segment supports observation and fault presence checking only.
Do not invent fault meanings, corrective actions, or additional controls not shown in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Overall System RMS or Geek+ RMS web page
Ability to view the RMS screen directly or through someone on the support call

access_required: Access to the Overall System RMS page, or access to a person on the support call who can view it
system_preconditions: The Overall System RMS page is available for viewing in the Geek+ RMS web page

visual_references.description: Training video frame showing the Overall System RMS page and describing that system faults appear on the left and active AGV faults on the right.

steps.title: Ask whether there are any system faults
steps.purpose: Establish the initial fault status at the start of support troubleshooting.
steps.instruction: Begin the support check by asking whether there are any system faults.
steps.expected_result: The caller confirms whether system faults are currently present or visible.
steps.healthy_condition: No system faults are reported or visible.
steps.failure_condition: System faults are reported or visible, or the caller cannot confirm the current fault state.

actions.label: Ask initial fault question
actions.exact_action: Ask: do you have any system faults?

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The training frame and transcript note that asking about system faults should be the first question.

stop_or_escalate_if: The caller cannot confirm whether any system faults are present
No one on the call can access or view the Overall System RMS page

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'Do you have any system faults, right? Should be the first question.'

steps.title: View the Overall System RMS page
steps.purpose: Open or obtain visibility to the page that displays system faults and active AGV faults.
steps.instruction: Access or view the Overall System RMS page associated with the Geek+ RMS web page. If you do not have direct access, use a person on the support call who can view the page.
steps.expected_result: The Overall System RMS page is visible for review.
steps.healthy_condition: The page is available and can be reviewed by support or by someone on the call.
steps.failure_condition: The page cannot be accessed or no one on the call can view it.

actions.label: Open or obtain view of Overall System RMS
actions.exact_action: Access or view the Overall System RMS page associated with the Geek+ RMS web page.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall System RMS page reference showing this is the Geek+ RMS web page view used for fault review.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Overall System RMS page is not available for viewing
No participant on the support call has access to the page

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR: 'Overall System RMS ... This page will show the Geek+ RMS web page ...'
The training segment references an Overall System RMS page associated with the Geek+ RMS web page.

steps.title: Check the left side for system faults
steps.purpose: Determine whether system faults are shown in the system faults area of the page.
steps.instruction: Review the left side of the Overall System RMS page for system faults.
steps.expected_result: You determine whether system faults are shown on the left side of the page.
steps.healthy_condition: No system faults are shown on the left side.
steps.failure_condition: System faults are shown on the left side, or the left-side fault area cannot be reviewed.

actions.label: Inspect left-side system faults area
actions.exact_action: Check the left side of the Overall System RMS page for system faults.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The left side of the Overall System RMS page where system faults are shown.

stop_or_escalate_if: System faults are present and need further diagnosis outside this source segment
The left-side system faults area is not visible or cannot be confirmed

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR: '... will also show system faults on the left ...'
System faults are displayed on the left side of the Overall System RMS page.

steps.title: Check the right side for active AGV faults
steps.purpose: Determine whether active AGV faults are shown in the AGV fault area of the page.
steps.instruction: Review the right side of the Overall System RMS page for any active AGV faults.
steps.expected_result: You determine whether active AGV faults are shown on the right side of the page.
steps.healthy_condition: No active AGV faults are shown on the right side.
steps.failure_condition: Active AGV faults are shown on the right side, or the right-side AGV fault area cannot be reviewed.

actions.label: Inspect right-side active AGV faults area
actions.exact_action: Check the right side of the Overall System RMS page for any active AGV faults.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The right side of the Overall System RMS page where active AGV faults are shown.

stop_or_escalate_if: Active AGV faults are present and require diagnosis beyond this source segment
The right-side active AGV faults area is not visible or cannot be confirmed

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR: '... and any active AGV faults on the right.'
Active AGV faults are displayed on the right side of the Overall System RMS page.

steps.title: Use another participant's view if direct access is unavailable
steps.purpose: Work around screen-sharing or HMI access limitations during the support call.
steps.instruction: If you do not have direct access, ask another person on the support call who has access to show the screen or describe what is visible on the Overall System RMS page.
steps.expected_result: Fault visibility can still be confirmed through another participant on the call.
steps.healthy_condition: Someone on the call can view or share the page and report what is shown.
steps.failure_condition: No one on the call can access, share, or reliably describe the page contents.

actions.label: Request shared view or verbal confirmation
actions.exact_action: Have another person on the support call show the screen or share what is visible on the page if they have access.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The source frame associated with the discussion of using available screen access during support calls.

stop_or_escalate_if: Many callers do not have HMI screen-sharing access and no alternate viewer is available
The information being relayed from the page is incomplete or cannot be trusted

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript summary: sometimes someone on a level 1 support call can share their screen, but many callers may not have access to share the HMI screen.
Some level 1 support calls may include screen sharing, but many callers at these sites do not have HMI screen-sharing access.

success_criteria: The Overall System RMS page is reviewed directly or through a participant on the support call.
It is confirmed whether system faults are present on the left side of the page.
It is confirmed whether active AGV faults are present on the right side of the page.
healthy_conditions: No system faults are shown on the left side of the Overall System RMS page.
No active AGV faults are shown on the right side of the Overall System RMS page.
A participant on the support call can access or describe the page when needed.
failure_conditions: System faults are shown on the left side of the page.
Active AGV faults are shown on the right side of the page.
No one on the support call can access, share, or confirm the Overall System RMS page.
escalation_guidance: Escalate when faults are present and further interpretation or corrective action is required, because this source only supports identifying where faults are shown.
Escalate when access limitations prevent anyone on the call from viewing or confirming the Overall System RMS page.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall System RMS page showing system faults on the left and active AGV faults on the right.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'Do you have any system faults, right? Should be the first question.' OCR: 'Overall System RMS ... This page will show the Geek+ RMS web page ... will also show system faults on the left and any active AGV faults on the right.'

missing_details: The source does not provide navigation steps for reaching the Overall System RMS page.
The source does not define fault severity, interpretation, or corrective actions.
The source does not provide escalation contacts or routing details.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing this check.
